title: 90 Day Fiance — Topical Risk Decision — Phase 16.10

page_under_review.in_sitemap: UNKNOWN — likely not (if not in posts.ts)
page_under_review.factual_status: EFFECTIVELY DELETED — not present in blog content source (posts.ts). URL returns Next.js 404 page with HTTP 200.

evaluation.finance_relevance: ZERO — 90 Day Fiance is a reality TV show with zero financial/calculator relevance
evaluation.topical_authority_dilution: SEVERE — entertainment/reality TV content dilutes QFINHUB's financial calculator authority signal
evaluation.adsense_risk: MODERATE — reality TV content could trigger 'low quality content' signals if indexed
evaluation.current_index_status: EFFECTIVELY DEAD — page not in posts.ts, returns 404 content, Google likely already dropped it
evaluation.should_noindex: N/A — page already effectively removed from blog system

auditor_review.reasoning: Page does not exist in content source. Returns 404 page content. Google's natural crawl will remove it. No manual noindex needed — Google will see the 404 title and drop it from index. Adding noindex to a non-existent page is unnecessary overhead.
auditor_review.risk_of_keeping_dead: NONE — page already gone. If Google still has it indexed (unlikely since it returns 404 content), natural crawl will drop it within weeks.

technical_guardian_review.reasoning: No code changes needed. Page not in posts.ts — the source of truth. Next.js serves 404 for unknown blog slugs. No DNS risk, no canonical risk, no redirect risk. The system is already handling this correctly.

decision: HOLD — page already effectively removed. No noindex needed. Monitor GSC for any residual indexed status. Add to off-topic watchlist for future monitoring.
decision_option: HOLD_UNTIL_10+_IMPRESSIONS — but in this case, page is dead, so effectively KEEP_INDEXED is not applicable and NOINDEX_APPROVED is unnecessary.
next_action: Add to off-topic page watchlist. Verify not in sitemap. No code changes required.
